key_issues: APPROVED. All iteration-001 criticals (C1-C5) and suggestions (S4, S5) are genuinely resolved and re-verified against the delivered spec-2025-11-25.rkt / spec-2026-07-28.rkt structs.

C1 resolved: Group 4 split into 4a (client params -> facade-request-meta) / 4b (server->client + create-message -> plain meta) / 4c (2025-only). Verified elicit params have NO task/meta in 2026 so both are 2025-only; create-message meta is plain (opt/c json-object?) in both revs + task 2025-only.

C2 resolved: per-primitive meta-field type stated with a column + acceptance criterion + Part 1 step 6 type assertions (2026 call-tool meta is facade-request-meta?; 2026 create-message meta NOT facade-request-meta?).

C3 resolved: new Testing Part 0 enumerates the six hand-authored fixtures (list-roots both revs, task-bearing 2025 tools/call, tool with execution, elicit-params, inputResponses-bearing tools/call) with the 2025-only-field absence assertions made HARD; correctly flags 2026-input-responses.json as a map not a params fixture.

C4 resolved: result `rest` is shared, not revision-gated, passes through on denormalize to either revision, never refused; mandatory home-revision rest-survival round-trip + empty-rest-is-{}-not-absent + cross-revision-pass-through tests added.

C5 resolved: ElicitResult result-type confirmed 2026-only against code; elicit-RESULT (meta/rest shared) vs elicit-PARAMS (meta 2025-only) explicitly distinguished; 'no open questions remain' note added.
S4 resolved: dispatch is (dispatch-for method revision) with a mandatory both-revisions tools/call collision test asserting two different parser/normalizer pairs.

S5 resolved: Group-0 must convert both revisions into the one chosen façade struct type (pure aliasing alone breaks the SAME-façade claim); guard test added. Minors (tool-exec accessor, Group-6 garbled fragment) fixed and verified.

Non-blocking nits only: (1) Group-3 field table omits the 2025 list-roots-result `rest` field (operationally covered by the 'emit exactly {roots}' rule, so no wrong build); (2) suggest an explicit assertion that a 2025 _meta progressToken is extracted into facade-request-meta-progress-token (currently covered loosely by 'survive from both').
